items.title: Aave fights court-ordered $73 million ETH freeze, argues ‘a thief does not own what he steals’
items.desc: Aave LLC filed an emergency motion in federal court seeking to lift an order that froze ether tied to last month's Kelp DAO exploit.

items.title: South Korea’s largest crypto exchange Upbit launches Ethereum blockchain with Optimism Foundation support
items.desc: Upbit is the Optimism Foundation's first client on its "self-managed" enterprise tier, giving it control over its core rollup architecture.

items.title: Binance adds protection against physical threats as problems with crypto wrench attacks persist
items.desc: Blockchain analytics firm CertiK said this February that attacks using physical threats to steal crypto rose 75% in 2025.

items.title: Banks Stay Tight-Lipped as Senators Reveal Proposed Clarity Act Stablecoin Deal
items.desc: Senators unveiled a Clarity Act compromise on the subject of stablecoin rewards. Crypto seems happy enough, but what about the banks?

items.title: Glamsterdam Upgrade Set To Triple Ethereum's Execution Capacity
items.desc: ePBS, Block-Level Access Lists, and EIP-8037 repricings combine to unlock a 200M gas limit floor, a throughput jump that could keep network fees pinned for years.

items.title: Western Union Launches USDPT Stablecoin on Solana via Anchorage Digital
items.desc: Remittance giant Western Union’s USDPT token will power cross-border payments and a consumer spending product launching in 40+ countries.

items.title: World Liberty Financial Sues Justin Sun for Defamation
items.desc: The move is the latest in an escalating feud and comes just weeks after Sun filed a lawsuit against World Liberty Financial for freezing his WLFI, alongside other allegations.

items.title: Bitcoin bounces around $80K as Iran strike unsettles crypto, risk assets
items.desc: Bitcoin faced new volatility and a new struggle to reclaim $80,000 as Iran events added pressure to crypto and risk assets.

items.title: Coinbase-incubated Base network adopts Succinct’s zero-knowledge tech
items.desc: Base, launched as an optimistic rollup, is hybridizing its tech stack with Succinct's SP1 zero-knowledge virtual machine.

items.title: Prediction markets entering institutional era after first block trade — Bernstein
items.desc: Institutional investors are entering prediction markets as block trades, custom contracts and US regulatory shifts reshape a sector still dominated by retail users.

items.title: Strive’s (ASST) Bitcoin Treasury Crosses 15,000 BTC After $33.9 Million Purchase
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Strive’s (ASST) Bitcoin Treasury Crosses 15,000 BTC After $33.9 Million Purchase Strive, Inc. (Nasdaq: ASST) said Monday its Bitcoin treasury has topped 15,000 BTC after it acquired 444 bitcoin for $33.9 million at an average price of $76,307 per coin. This post Strive’s (ASST) Bitcoin Treasury Crosses 15,000 BTC After $33.9 Million Purchase first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: SEC Delays Rollout of ETFs Tied to Prediction Markets: Reuters
items.desc: The SEC extended its review period for ETFs that would have tracked prediction market odds on political races and economic indicators.

items.title: DTCC Sets July Launch Window for Tokenized Securities Pilot
items.desc: The DTC unit will begin processing limited tokenized trades in July before opening the service more broadly in October.

items.title: Ethereum holders are back in profit as ETH price chart targets $3K
items.desc: Ethereum investors are no longer in the red, increasing the chances of a rally to $3,000, but resistance at $2,800 may delay recovery.

items.title: The government should promote innovation, not punish it
items.desc: District Attorney of Sacramento County, Ho argues that ambiguous rules and enforcement practices, such as those for crypto, lead to confusion, rather than seeking clarity in the law to further growth.

items.title: Aave Asks Court to Vacate Restraining Notice Targeting Recovered Kelp DAO Assets
items.desc: The emergency motion challenges a New York court order redirecting recovered Kelp DAO exploit funds toward decades-old terrorism judgments against North Korea.

items.title: Shopify and National Bank of Canada are among backers of a new digital currency built to settle trades 24/7
items.desc: Tetra Trust's new stablecoin, CADD, is aimed at institutional use for 24/7 cross-border settlement, real-time corporate treasury, and direct fintech transfers, replacing legacy batch systems.

items.title: DTC to begin tokenized asset rollout in July ahead of full launch as it gets feedback from BlackRock, Circle and others
items.desc: The Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation will roll out its tokenization service as it gathers feedback from firms like BlackRock to Circle.

items.title: SEC pumps the brakes on prediction market ETFs set to debut this week: Reuters
items.desc: The prediction market ETFs covered U.S. election outcomes, layoffs in the tech sector and the liklihood of a recession.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.desc: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: SEC delays prediction market ETFs over mechanics and risk concerns: Report
items.desc: The regulator reportedly requested more information from Roundhill, GraniteShares and Bitwise about how the event contract funds would work.

items.title: Lawyer Attempts to Seize Frozen ETH Linked to Kelp Exploit From Arbitrum DAO
items.desc: A U.S. attorney is attempting to claim ETH frozen by Arbitrum following the Kelp DAO exploit — on behalf of victims of North Korean state hackers from prior incidents.

items.title: BitMine Buys $240 Million in Ethereum as Tom Lee Heralds Arrival of 'Crypto Spring'
items.desc: Tom Lee's BitMine Immersion Technologies made its third straight weekly Ethereum buy above 100K ETH as the coin continues rising.

items.title: Wall Street giant DTCC plans tokenized securities platform with July pilot, October launch
items.desc: Clearing giant's move toward live tokenization market adds weight to Wall Street's blockchain push.

items.title: Western Union launches USDPT stablecoin issued by Anchorage on Solana
items.desc: Western Union launched a dollar stablecoin on Solana for 24/7 settlement across 200+ countries, the company said Monday.

items.title: Kraken’s parent company alleges custodian Etana misappropriated $25 million
items.desc: Etana is accused of operating a "Ponzi-like enterprise" the resulted in using capital to fund expenses and risky investments.

items.title: Hut 8 refinances Bitcoin-backed loan with $200M FalconX deal
items.desc: The Bitcoin miner said the FalconX facility lowers its fixed interest rate to 7% and releases about 3,300 BTC from collateral restrictions.

items.title: Strategy Skips Bitcoin Buy as BTC Hits $80K for First Time Since January
items.desc: Bitcoin just hit its highest price in months, right as $65 billion treasury firm Strategy took a rare week off from BTC accumulation.

items.title: BitMine adds 101,745 ETH for $238 million in third straight weekly buy above 100,000 tokens
items.desc: BitMine Immersion's latest weekly buy pushes total ether holdings to 5.18 million tokens worth around $12.1 billion.

items.title: Tom Lee says 'crypto spring' started as largest Ethereum treasury buys $238 million in ether
items.desc: Bitmine's latest ETH purchase comes as Lee cited progress on digital asset regulation CLARITY Act as a key catalyst for crypto markets.

items.title: Stablecoin startup Rain joins Mastercard as a Principal Member
items.desc: Rain, which was valued at $1.95 billion during a Series C round announced in January, has an existing relationship with card issuer Visa.

items.title: Binance is launching a withdrawal lock to help deter crypto wrench attacks
items.desc: The lock is meant to protect users from being forced into withdrawing their funds, though it's an internal policy and not a cryptographic lock.

items.title: GameStop Makes $55.5 Billion Offer to Acquire eBay
items.desc: GameStop has built a 5% stake in eBay and proposed to fund the deal with cash on hand plus third-party financing.

items.title: Bitcoin price eyes $96K as institutions absorb 500% of daily BTC supply
items.desc: In past instances, BTC has averaged 24% gains in one month when institutional demand absorbed over 500% of the daily mined supply.

items.title: Morning Minute: Bitcoin Clears $80K, Reverses on Iran Missile Strike
items.desc: Bitcoin rallied above $80,000 overnight before selling off on reports of a U.S. warship struck by Iranian missiles. Plus, Clarity Act odds spiked after a new compromise on stablecoin yield.

items.title: A beginner’s guide to stablecoins
items.desc: From pegs to reserves to regulation, here's what you need to know ahead of Consensus Miami 2026.

items.title: Bitcoin scaling layer Citrea rolls out CTR token and dual treasury model
items.desc: Only xCTR used for voting will earn additional liquidity emissions, while staked CTR that is inactive will only earn unstaking penalty fees.

items.title: Kraken's parent company Payward alleges $25 million crypto custody fraud in lawsuit against Etana and firm's CEO
items.desc: The crypto exchange claims client funds were misused, commingled and concealed in a “Ponzi-like” scheme that unraveled amid a liquidity crisis.

items.title: South Korea crypto sector warns AML proposal goes too far: Report
items.desc: Crypto industry body DAXA said the proposed rules could push suspicious transaction reports from South Korea’s five largest exchanges to more than 5.4 million a year, Yonhap reported.

items.title: SOL Strategies to acquire cross-chain swap aggregator Houdini for $18 million
items.desc: The acquisition is expected to create a new revenue stream and make SOL Strategies less reliant on market cycles.

items.title: Kraken parent Payward closes $550 million Bitnomial deal, securing full CFTC derivatives stack
items.desc: The deal gives Payward a full CFTC-regulated derivatives stack in the U.S.

items.title: Laywer pops up on Arbitrum DAO forums seeking funds for victims of decades-old North Korean terrorist acts
items.desc: Families holding decades-old judgments against North Korea are trying to seize 30,765 ETH frozen after last month’s rsETH exploit, citing alleged links between the attack and DPRK-linked hacking groups such as Lazarus, and invoking a New York restraining notice that could block Arbitrum from releasing the funds.

items.title: Western Union Stablecoin USDPT Launches on Solana
items.desc: Western Union's USDPT stablecoin has gone live on the Solana blockchain, marking the traditional remittance giant's entry into on-chain digital assets.

items.title: Iran missile report sends bitcoin back to $79,000, with ETH, SOL, DOGE sharply lower
items.desc: The largest crypto reversed sharply from a $80,594 high after Iran's Fars news agency claimed two missiles hit a U.S. warship, with oil spiking 5% before the U.S. denied the report.

items.title: GameStop makes $55.5B offer to buy eBay in surprise takeover bid
items.desc: GameStop proposes a $55.5 billion cash-and-stock takeover of eBay, disclosing a 5% stake and says CEO Ryan Cohen would lead the combined company.

items.title: Coinbase boosts Solana trading with DFlow integration
items.desc: Coinbase adding DFlow as its primary router will mean eight times less trade failures.

items.title: Crypto bears got it wrong again, losing $300 million in liquidations
items.desc: Bitcoin's move to $80,000 caught bears on the wrong side of the market again, leading to large short liquidations.

items.title: Capital B raises $1.3M from Adam Back for Bitcoin strategy
items.desc: The Blockstream CEO subscribed to 10 million warrants as Capital B pushes ahead with its Bitcoin treasury strategy.

items.title: North Korea blames ‘reptile media’ over reports of crypto theft
items.desc: North Korea rejected U.S. cybercrime claims as 'absurd slander,' blaming media as crypto theft attributed to the country exceeded $6 billion.

items.title: US law firm attempts to block transfer of frozen ETH from Kelp exploit
items.desc: Gerstein Harrow has filed similar cases in the past, arguing its clients have a claim to funds stolen by the DPRK and frozen by crypto firms.

items.title: Jobs data, earnings calls: Crypto Week Ahead
items.desc: Your look at what's coming in the week starting May 4.

items.title: Recovery in bitcoin ETF inflows is real. It is just not complete yet.
items.desc: While ETF inflows have resumed, the recovery has yet to match last fall’s peak.

items.title: Kraken parent Payward completes Bitnomial acquisition, unlocking US crypto derivatives offering
items.desc: With the deal closed, Payward now holds a full suite of U.S. derivatives licenses issued by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission.

items.title: Bitcoin climbs past $80K for the first time since January
items.desc: Bitcoin soared as the MSCI AC Asia Index rose to a new high on Monday, an early indicator that investors viewed the weekend developments in a reasonably positive light.

items.title: CFTC sees mixed responses to prediction market rulemaking
items.desc: The CFTC received more than 1,500 responses to its prediction market rulemaking proposal, with respondents divided on how it should police the platforms.

items.title: Strategy raised $82 million last week but holds off on bitcoin buys ahead of earnings
items.desc: Michael Saylor says the company will resume purchases next week, but the pause lands as analysts focus on losses and the growing complexity of Strategy’s preferred-stock funding machine.

items.title: The Funding: How DeFi hacks could hit crypto funds
items.desc: DeFi’s latest hack wave is likely to add pressure on liquid and yield-focused crypto funds already navigating a tough market.

items.title: ‘Stablecoins’ are an outdated term from crypto’s early years: A16z
items.desc: John Palmer, a developer and brand adviser, agreed, said it "feels like a bug" to call them stablecoins and that they should have a self-defined and non-reactionary name.

items.title: Veteran trader Peter Brandt sees bitcoin hitting $250,000, but only after a bottom later this year
items.desc: Veteran trader Peter Brandt sees bitcoin rallying to $250,000 in 2029, but only after the market finishes a long drawn-out bottoming process that could last into September 2026.

items.title: XRP zooms above $1.40 on rising volume, putting focus on further breakout
items.desc: Sharp volume surge drives move through $1.40, meaning traders may now watch whether the level holds as support.

items.title: Bitcoin reclaims $80,000 as flows build, but traders hedge and doubt a breakout
items.desc: Strong ETF inflows and rising leverage are lifting prices, yet CryptoQuant data shows weak spot demand and Polymarket odds put just a 23% chance on $90,000 this month.

items.title: Stablecoin firms have a $112B additional opportunity in LATAM remittance
items.desc: The US-to-Mexico remittance corridor, while still the largest, shrank 4.5% in 2025 as other Latin American corridors grew.

items.title: Michael Saylor’s Strategy pauses weekly bitcoin purchases ahead of Q1 earnings release
items.desc: Strategy's Q1 earnings report is set to release on Tuesday, with Wall Street expecting a $18.98 loss per share.

items.title: Strategy takes Bitcoin buying breather ahead of Q1 earnings report
items.desc: Michael Saylor signaled a “pause” on BTC buying ahead of Tuesday’s earnings report, with Wall Street expecting a loss for Q1.

items.title: How Canton Network Lets Institutions Guard Against DeFi Security Risks: Digital Asset CEO
items.desc: Because Canton allows participants to implement guardrails, Digital Asset's Yuval Rooz said institutions can protect against bad actors.

items.title: Bitcoin preps highest weekly close since January as BTC price nears $79K
items.desc: Bitcoin canceled out the week's earlier losses to tease the highest weekly BTC price candle close since the end of January near $79,000.

items.title: Iran’s largest crypto exchange founded by sons of family tied to supreme leaders: Reuters
items.desc: Nobitex, Iran’s largest crypto exchange, was founded by brothers linked to the powerful Kharrazi family, which has ties to the country’s supreme leaders.

items.title: US CISA adds ‘insane’ Linux Copy Fail flaw to watch list
items.desc: Malicious actors with code execution capability may gain root access on Linux systems using as few as 10 lines of Python, according to a researcher.

items.title: New York forces Uphold to pay $5M over fraudulent crypto investment scheme
items.desc: New York AG Letitia James secured a $5 million settlement from Uphold for promoting CredEarn, a crypto savings product that misled users about its risks.

items.title: Crypto industry will be ‘just fine’ if CLARITY Act doesn’t pass: Chris Perkins
items.desc: The efforts of the SEC and CFTC chairs indicate that the crypto industry will not suffer without the CLARITY Act, according to crypto executive Chris Perkins.

items.title: Bitcoin logs best monthly performance in 12 months during April
items.desc: Despite being the best-performing month in the past 12 months, Bitcoin still came in slightly below its historical average, according to CoinGlass data.

items.title: Wallets seized by OFAC may not be Iranian; other state actors instead: Analyst
items.desc: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that wallets the US targeted as part of Operation Economic Fury were linked to Tehran, but analysis of the wallets' characteristics suggests otherwise.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.
